prompt: The UP008 rule (`super-call-with-parameters`) in Ruff incorrectly triggers inside nested classes. When a nested class like `class Outer: class Inner(Base): ...` has a method calling `super(Inner, self).__init__()`, UP008 suggests removing the parameters to use bare `super()`. However, inside a nested class, referencing `Inner` by name is actually a `NameError` at runtime because the inner class name is not in scope within its own methods. Therefore, UP008 should NOT fire when the `super()` call references the inner class name inside a nested class context. Fix the rule implementation in `crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/pyupgrade/rules/super_call_with_parameters.rs` to detect when the class being passed to `super()` is a nested class and skip the diagnostic in that case. Update the test fixture and snapshot accordingly.
